title: Hieracium amaurocymum
intro: Hieracium amaurocymum, also known as spotted hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, spotted hawkweed has been used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium amaurocymum include sesquiterpene lactones, such as helenalin and iso-helenalin, as well as other flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with spotted hawkweed include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
